text.title: Positioning Gate Loop and Canvas Maintenance
text.summary: Defines the PM's positioning-fit gate for every backlog item, quarterly canvas refresh triggers, drift detection across quarters, and agent-specific failure modes.

section.title: Positioning-Fit Gate

rule.statement: Score every backlog item 0-3 on positioning-fit before it enters a sprint: 0 = orthogonal or dilutes, 1 = neutral table-stakes, 2 = reinforces one unique attribute, 3 = reinforces two or more. Veto items scoring 0 regardless of RICE score.
rule.rationale: RICE scoring inflates Reach and ignores positioning-fit; a positioning-diluting feature can rank high on RICE while eroding the product's differentiation quarter by quarter.

rule.statement: Require a citation (canvas section + telemetry signal) for any positioning-fit score of 2 or higher; reject uncited scores.
rule.rationale: Backlog authors learn the gate's heuristics and stuff PRDs with positioning keywords; requiring a citation from the actual canvas prevents score gaming.

rule.statement: Include a "positioning fit" column alongside RICE in roadmap reviews; add an "off-positioning shipped" anti-metric (trend-down target) to the product health dashboard.
rule.rationale: What gets measured gets managed; tracking off-positioning features shipped creates organizational pressure to maintain the positioning discipline.

section.reference: Flag backlog items that dilute positioning — non-zero exit on dilution detected

rule.statement: Refresh the positioning canvas at the start of each planning quarter; diff it against the prior quarter and run a backlog-impact report for in-flight initiatives.
rule.rationale: Incremental drift is invisible in any single quarter; a diff across four quarters shows the trajectory of the positioning — and whether the wedge is being maintained or lost.

rule.statement: Do not change the canvas if the diff would invalidate more than 50% of the in-flight roadmap without PM sign-off; output a "breaking-change" report instead.
rule.rationale: A canvas change that invalidates most of the in-flight work is a strategic pivot, not a positioning refresh; the PM must explicitly acknowledge it before any downstream doc updates.

rule.statement: Run the outbound-loop propagator when the canvas changes: update roadmap.md, OKR docs, pricing-page copy, and sales enablement skeletons atomically in a single PR.
rule.rationale: A canvas updated without propagating to OKRs and roadmap creates the classic misalignment: quarterly OKRs still reference last quarter's positioning while the product has moved on.

antipattern.description: Agent regenerating the canvas without checking whether the in-flight backlog can deliver the new positioning.
antipattern.reason: Canvas-first, backlog-second sequencing creates a positioning that the current development capacity cannot deliver; every regen prompt must include the in-flight roadmap.

antipattern.description: Agent widening the canvas to absorb every lost-deal feature request (sales-laundered positioning).
antipattern.reason: Feature-list widening destroys differentiation; pre-process win/loss notes to extract the category of competitor, not feature checklists, and reject feature-list dilution at the prompt level.

antipattern.description: Agent generating positioning sections with "customer-centric approach," "best-in-class," or "innovative" as unique attributes.
antipattern.reason: These terms are claims every competitor makes; require a specific named attribute from the canvas and a citation that differentiates it from direct competitors.

antipattern.description: PRD positioning section auto-filled with generic text like "this reinforces our customer-centric approach" without naming a specific canvas attribute.
antipattern.reason: Generic positioning answers are worse than no answer; they pass the gate-loop check superficially while adding no information, and they train reviewers to stop reading the positioning section.

list.item: PM approves the regenerated canvas before any downstream doc updates.
PM signs off on each ship/defer/reject recommendation from the gate-loop agent — agents must not auto-close issues.
PM personally walks marketing, sales, and support through canvas changes once per quarter.
PM owns the call on category-creation vs. subcategory positioning — agents flag the question, never decide it.
